ogTitle: Swans Crossing vs Swansea Heads: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Swans Crossing and Swansea Heads.
heading: Swans Crossing vs Swansea Heads — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Swans Crossing, New South Wales and Swansea Heads, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Swans Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Swansea Heads (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Swans Crossing or Swansea Heads safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Swans Crossing has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Swansea Heads scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Swans Crossing scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. Swansea Heads scores 20/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Swans Crossing or Swansea Heads?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Swans Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
